title: _elementtree.TreeBuilder: concurrent feed of one shared builder races its unsynchronized parse state (self->data / self->last / self->this / self->last_for_tail / self->index / self->stack); treebuilder_handle_* have no lock / no critical section
description: A TreeBuilder keeps all parse state in plain TreeBuilderObject struct fields. Every public feed method (start/data/end/comment/pi -> treebuilder_handle_*, plus the shared helper treebuilder_flush_data / treebuilder_extend_element_text_or_tail) mutates those fields in place -- Py_CLEAR/Py_SETREF/Py_NewRef on the pointer fields and index++/index-- on the stack cursor -- with no lock and no @critical_section (Modules/_elementtree.c defines zero Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION, on the confirmed build and on current main). Two threads calling any feed methods on the SAME shared builder therefore data-race on the builder's internals. This is a cluster of ~8 treebuilder_* vs treebuilder_* signatures (~18 vehicles in fleet-03), all faces of the same unsynchronized shared-builder parse-state bug. Representative face: treebuilder_flush_data reads `if (!self->data)` (:2684) while treebuilder_extend_element_text_or_tail writes self->data NULL (:2636/:2644).
sites: Modules/_elementtree.c treebuilder_flush_data:2684 (READ self->data: `if (!self->data)`; called at top of handle_start/_end/_comment/_pi)
Modules/_elementtree.c treebuilder_extend_element_text_or_tail:2636 (WRITE self->data = NULL: `*data = NULL` after JOIN_SET; data == &self->data) [this repro's write face]
Modules/_elementtree.c treebuilder_extend_element_text_or_tail:2644 (WRITE self->data = NULL: `Py_CLEAR(*data)`) [seeding fleet vehicle's write face]
Modules/_elementtree.c treebuilder_handle_end:2872 (WRITE self->this + READ self->stack/self->index: `self->this = Py_NewRef(PyList_GET_ITEM(self->stack, self->index))`)
Modules/_elementtree.c treebuilder_handle_start:2790/2798 (READ/WRITE self->index + self->stack: index bound check, PyList_SetItem/Append, self->index++)
race_kind: shared builtin object's internal parse state: multiple non-atomic reader/writer call sites (refcount RMW on PyObject* fields + non-atomic Py_ssize_t stack cursor) with no per-object critical section; cluster of treebuilder_* vs treebuilder_* faces
shared_state: TreeBuilderObject parse-state fields on a single shared _elementtree.TreeBuilder: data (pending text/tail collector), last, this, last_for_tail (current/most-recent nodes), index (stack cursor) and stack (open-element list)
severity: moderate (crash-capable in principle; no crash observed -- TSan halts on the plain-memory race first). Confirmed report is a pointer read/write on self->data, but the surrounding unsynchronized ops are refcount RMW (Py_CLEAR/Py_SETREF/Py_NewRef/Py_DECREF) on shared PyObject* fields (UAF/double-decref risk) and a non-atomic self->index++/-- that indexes self->stack (OOB risk). Conventionally single-threaded object (parser sink) lowers real-world priority; but declares Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED and shared builtin should not data-race (Yhg1s ruling).
repro_reliability: deterministic: exit 66 on 8/8 runs within ~1s; SUMMARY names different faces of the cluster across runs (most often flush_data/extend_element_text_or_tail on self->data; also handle_start/handle_end on self->this). No XMLParser/expat -- bare TreeBuilder isolates the builder-internal race.
suggested_fix: Apply the module's own _lock_held split-function + Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self) pattern to the WHOLE TreeBuilder feed path: wrap each public clinic method (_elementtree_TreeBuilder_start/_data/_end/_comment/_pi/_close) in a per-object critical section on self so flush_data + the field mutations run atomically per builder; make treebuilder_flush_data/treebuilder_extend_element_text_or_tail _lock_held helpers. Per-object critical section on self is the right mechanism (state is per-builder). ALL feed methods must take it -- the abandoned PR gh-145569 (REQ-7) locked only treebuilder_handle_end, which is insufficient (handle_start/_data/_comment/_pi still race on self->data/self->last/self->this).
confirmed_unfixed_on: current main: Modules/_elementtree.c last touched by 7de4fcd44585f572acbcee23f5c7018b2b3f0983 (gh-149571) contains zero Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION -- feed path still fully unlocked, race live
notes: NOT covered by any merged fix. Closest prior art: issue gh-145568 / PR gh-145569 ('Fix thread safety in _elementtree.c', audited for gh-116738) -- CLOSED, PR never merged; its REQ-7 identified ONE facet ('treebuilder_handle_end reads self->stack and decrements self->index without a lock') and proposed a _lock_held split, but was abandoned and would not fix the self->data/self->last/self->this faces. Umbrella gh-149816 ('22 free-threading race conditions') lists two _elementtree items -- (69) Element.__len__ (PR gh-149918, open) and (87) Element.text -- both Element-object races, NOT the TreeBuilder feed-method race; this cluster is not in its enumerated list. gh-116738 (audit built-in modules for thread safety): squarely in remit. Distinct from TSAN-0009 (pyexpat/expat parser -- this needs no XMLParser/expat; one vehicle's stdout printed an expat_malloc race first under halt_on_error, a different bug firing first in a mixed vehicle) and from TSAN-0013/TSAN-0022 (_setevents aliasing a shared LIST / parser events fields -- this is the builder's own data/last/this/index/stack, not a list).

related_issues.note: CLOSED issue 'Fix thread safety in _elementtree.c' (audited for gh-116738); PR gh-145569 never merged. REQ-7 identified the treebuilder_handle_end stack/index face -- one facet of this cluster -- but was abandoned and only locked handle_end (insufficient for the data/last/this faces).

CLOSED (unmerged) PR for gh-145568. Applied _lock_held + Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION to Element paths and REQ-7 to treebuilder_handle_end only. Its approach is the correct fix template but must be extended to ALL treebuilder feed methods.
Audit all built-in modules for thread safety -- the FT-hardening remit this falls under; _elementtree declares Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED but leaves the TreeBuilder feed path unlocked.

'22 free-threading race conditions' umbrella. Lists (69) Element.__len__ (PR gh-149918 open) and (87) Element.text -- both Element-object races, distinct from the TreeBuilder feed-method cluster. Cross-checked: this cluster is not enumerated there.

related: TSAN-0009 (pyexpat don't-share-the-parser -- DISTINCT: this is TreeBuilder-internal, no expat)
TSAN-0013 (shared-list nonatomic read vs resize -- DISTINCT: this is builder struct fields, not a list)
TSAN-0022 (_elementtree _setevents shared-list / parser events fields -- DISTINCT: not the events list/fields)
